session.name: MPI-4 LTX-2.3 — LoRA HUNT COMPLETE. Tested all 5 capability LoRAs on the Builder Pod: VBVR-V4 (i2v), VBVR-V1 (t2v), Singularity OmniCine, Soft_Enhance, + recapped Transition. VERDICT: ship config LOCKED = BASE distilled LTX-2.3 + prompt-contract + Soft_Enhance (MERGED). All reasoning/anatomy LoRAs DROPPED. Also nailed: stage-1=motion/stage-2=detail rule, audio/music control findings, timeline-prompt-works-on-base, t2v opening-pose is promptable. Created Cubric-Prompt card MPI-11 (recipe findings). Pod being DELETED (volume kept, 75GB).

goal.original: MPI-4 LTX-2.3: decide the capability-LoRA set for the ~25th-June release, biasing toward shipping a refined base. Move to a Builder Pod for fast iteration.
goal.summary: LoRA decision COMPLETE. Ship config locked: i2v + t2v = BASE distilled LTX-2.3 + the prompt-contract + Soft_Enhance (SCHEDULED TO MERGE into the model, 0.5-0.7, realism/face polish). DROPPED: VBVR-V4 (i2v — base already follows ordered prompts; marginal; trained on Sulphur-2 base mismatch), VBVR-V1 (t2v — inconsistent across scenes), Singularity OmniCine (degrades audio with no documented fix + unprompted ethnicity bias + 2.5GB + slower + needs different prompt syntax). DEFERRED: Transition (FL-only use, needs an effect-system decision Cubric Vision doesn't have yet). Remaining for next session: audio-drop GATE test, execute the Soft merge, optionally video-extend (stage-1-latent vs final-video source question).

completed: TESTED ALL 5 capability LoRAs fairly (each in its native prompt format + creator's recommended envelope). VERDICTS: Soft_Enhance=KEEP+merge (the one clean win — softer/more realistic face+skin+lighting, 344MB, always-on, no prompt change; visible only on CLOSE shots). VBVR-V4(i2v)=DROP. VBVR-V1(t2v)=DROP. Singularity=DROP. Transition=DEFER. Full reasons in spec + playbook research/lora-strength-law.md + tested-loras-versions.md.

ESTABLISHED the STAGE-1=MOTION / STAGE-2=DETAIL rule (proven via transition stage-2-only=no-effect, stage-1=effect; + garment morph being stage-2-only). Corrected a stale spec entry that wrongly said transition was stage-2-only. Motion LoRAs→stage-1, detail LoRAs→stage-2.

AUDIO/MUSIC control findings: 'no music' prose is IGNORED (no NAG/negative field); 'cinematic'/'editorial' style words PULL IN scored music; listing CONCRETE diegetic foley (footsteps, fabric) suppresses music + renders, while abstract 'room tone' does NOT render; the stage-1 sampler has audio_normalization_factors (all=1.0) as a real workflow-level lever. Rain renders only as ground-impact drops.

PROMPT-CONTRACT refinements: timeline syntax ([Scene&Style]/[Action Timeline] 0-Xs/[Camera Timeline]) WORKS ON THE BASE MODEL (respects time segments + camera cuts); t2v opening-pose is promptable ('looking away from camera'→back-to-camera start, rotates into described pose); duration must match step-density (~1-1.5s/step; too-long = start-hang + over-run); small handheld props (cigarette/glasses) hallucinate/teleport in motion; name which hand holds what + call free hand 'empty'.
CREATED Cubric-Prompt kanban card MPI-11 (todo) holding all prompt-shape findings to graduate into ltx-2.3.recipe.ts (draft, t2v-only → needs i2v mode). Living card. Committed.

BUILT the durable record: playbook research/tested-loras-versions.md (exact versions+base tested = re-test baseline); lora-strength-law.md verdicts; model-set.md status table. Memory trimmed to pointers. All committed across 3 repos (Cubric-Vision RunPod branch, mpi-ci main, Cubric-Prompt).
Confirmed: Kijai's 'distilled LoRA' = a SPEED/step-compression LoRA, NOT reasoning — explains why base already follows prompts + why 3rd-party reasoning LoRAs are redundant.
pending: ⚠️ WORKFLOW WAS NOT SAVED before Pod deletion. On the next Pod: reload template + set LoRA chain to Soft_Enhance=0.7, ALL dropped LoRAs (VBVR-V4, VBVR-V1, Singularity, Transition)=0.0, then SAVE the workflow this time. (Backslash path note: the 3 may show 'missing' on Linux reload — re-select from dropdown.)

AUDIO-DROP GATE test (next-session task 1): does the input-audio influence need a STRENGTH SLIDER or just a binary 'did user drop audio / not' gate (auto-enable at a fixed strength on file-present)? Open: does a fixed strength (e.g. 1.0) work on EVERY image, or do some need lower (→ keep slider)? NOT an on/off button — a user-dropped-audio-or-not gate.
EXECUTE the Soft_Enhance MERGE into the shipped model (next-session task 2): confirm merge recipe + final strength (0.5-0.7) + per-stage weight.

VIDEO EXTEND (next-session task 3, IF time — lengthy, likely needs its own Pod): can extend be a SEPARATE workflow → OPTIONAL operation? LTX extend pulls from STAGE-1 LATENT but can ALSO pull from an ALREADY-MADE/FINAL video — MUST test the difference (extend-from-stage-1-latent vs extend-from-final-video: quality, seams, practicality). Half-wired (IfElse 51/56 feed save-gate NOT sampler — sampler-side continue feed unverified).

Loose ends (only if fast): v3 i2v MOTION test (the 'better motion, attention-only' VBVR variant never tested — judge MOTION not prompt-following); music style-word test ('cinematic' vs 'documentary/candid' — does un-cinematic style kill music?); transition/FL once an effect-system path is chosen.

EFFECT SYSTEM brainstorm (separate, not 25th): many style/effect LoRAs exist (transition is one); Cubric Vision has no effect system. How to deliver toggle/effect LoRAs (switch vs operation vs effect-system). Own brainstorm.
RELEASE ~25th June: ship the refined base (i2v/t2v/FL + input-audio gate + merged Soft) — config now locked. Add extend only if it lands clean in time.
Mirror heretic-gemma-fp8 (anongecko) to own repo before shipping as a dep (supply-chain). Singularity mirror no longer needed (dropped).

next_action.description: Resume MPI-4 on a NEW Pod started off the PERSISTENT 75GB volume (pick a BETTER GPU card than the RTX PRO 4500 — extend is lengthy; more VRAM cuts the staging tax). The LoRA decision is DONE: ship = base + prompt-contract + merged Soft_Enhance. Reload the template, set LoRA chain to Soft=0.7 / all-dropped=0.0, SAVE the workflow. Then do the two next-session tasks: (1) audio-drop GATE test (slider vs binary gate), (2) execute the Soft merge. If fast, (3) start video-extend (test stage-1-latent vs final-video source). Read the spec §0b + the playbook research/ first.

key_decisions: SHIP CONFIG LOCKED: i2v + t2v = BASE distilled LTX-2.3 + prompt-contract + Soft_Enhance MERGED (0.5-0.7). NO reasoning/anatomy LoRAs.

VBVR (V4 i2v + V1 t2v) DROPPED — base already follows ordered prompts; marginal; V4 base-mismatched (Sulphur-2). Singularity DROPPED — audio degrade (no doc fix) + ethnicity bias + 2.5GB + slower. Both recorded with re-test triggers in tested-loras-versions.md for future new versions.
Soft_Enhance KEEP → MERGE. The one clean win. Realism/face/lighting polish, 344MB, always-on, visible only on CLOSE shots (a usage rule).
Transition DEFERRED — FL-only (substantial env/character change between start+end frame); delivery undecided (switch/operation/EFFECT); Cubric Vision has no effect system → own brainstorm.
STAGE-1=MOTION, STAGE-2=DETAIL (general LTX multi-stage rule). Motion LoRAs→stage-1, detail→stage-2.
Audio: 'no music' prose ignored; concrete foley list suppresses music; 'cinematic' pulls music; audio_normalization_factors (workflow) is the real lever. Music suppression still an open product item.

App multi-stage prompt-edit (verified in code): user editing prompt + re-running stage-2 reuses the stage-1 latent (no recompute) — clean way to fix stage-2 upscale morphs (skirt→pants) cheaply without touching motion. Real product strength.
constraints: COOPERATIVE LIVE SESSION: user drives ComfyUI + Pod shell; agent edits JSON / guides / runs rclone. Confirm user SAVED+CLOSED the ComfyUI tab before any disk JSON write.
Live Pod ops (create/Stop/Terminate) are USER-only; agent has NO usable SSH.

PERSISTENT volume kept (75GB, survives Pod deletion) — next Pod mounts it, zero re-download. Pick a BETTER GPU (the RTX PRO 4500 32GB had a heavy VRAM-staging tax: 8s clip ~162s stage-1 + ~200s stage-2). Volume may be region-locked — if a new Pod can't mount it, re-run install_models_ltx23.sh (fast, R2-backed).
A/B LoRAs via strength 0.0↔0.5, NEVER bypass-toggle (bypass = model reload → RAM-leak → OOM; crashed the Pod at 99% RAM this session; ComfyUI self-heals in-place, don't pkill).
Lock a GOOD base seed on a CLEAN image first, then A/B — judging a LoRA delta on a bad/morphing seed = noise on noise.
Test each LoRA in its NATIVE prompt format + check creator's recommended base/precision (mismatch → flat response, e.g. VBVR-v4/Sulphur, Singularity/fp8).
CFG locked at 1 (distilled). Node naming Input_*/Output_*. MPI custom nodes only in anything that ships (rgthree authoring-only). PYTHONUTF8=1 for local python.

resume_prompt: You are resuming MPI-4 (LTX 2.3 video model integration) in a fresh session. Read .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-4/handoffs/c3f8a1e7-2b9d-4f6a-8e13-7c5b9d04a2f1.json and use mpi-continue. COOPERATIVE LIVE session: the user drives ComfyUI + the Pod shell; you edit JSON / guide commands / run rclone. THE LoRA DECISION IS DONE — ship config is LOCKED: i2v + t2v = BASE distilled LTX-2.3 + the prompt-contract + Soft_Enhance MERGED into the model (0.5-0.7, a realism/face/lighting polish — the one LoRA that earned its keep; visible only on close shots). All reasoning/anatomy LoRAs were tested fairly and DROPPED: VBVR-V4 (i2v — base already follows ordered prompts; also trained on a mismatched Sulphur-2 base), VBVR-V1 (t2v — inconsistent across scenes), Singularity OmniCine (degrades audio with NO documented fix + an unprompted ethnicity bias + 2.5GB + slower). Transition is DEFERRED (FL-only, needs an effect-system decision Cubric Vision doesn't have yet). The durable record is in the Builder playbook research/ (tested-loras-versions.md = exact versions tested for future re-tests; lora-strength-law.md = verdicts) and the live log is the MPI-4 spec §0b. Prompt-shape findings went to Cubric-Prompt card MPI-11. ⚠️ THE WORKFLOW WAS NOT SAVED before the Pod was deleted — on the new Pod, reload the template, set the LoRA chain to Soft_Enhance=0.7 and ALL dropped LoRAs (VBVR-V4, VBVR-V1, Singularity, Transition)=0.0, and SAVE it this time. Start a NEW Pod off the PERSISTENT 75GB volume (it survives — pick a BETTER GPU than the RTX PRO 4500, since the next work is video-extend which is lengthy and more VRAM cuts the heavy staging tax). THEN do the next-session tasks IN ORDER: (1) AUDIO-DROP GATE — test whether the input-audio influence needs a strength SLIDER or just a binary 'did the user drop audio or not' gate (auto-enable at a fixed strength on file-present); the open question is whether one fixed strength works on every image or some need lower. (2) Execute the Soft_Enhance MERGE into the shipped model. (3) IF time, VIDEO EXTEND as an optional operation — the key test is extend-from-stage-1-latent vs extend-from-an-already-made-final-video (quality/seams/practicality), which decides the extend architecture; it's half-wired (IfElse 51/56 feed the save-gate not the sampler). Method reminders: A/B LoRAs via strength 0↔0.5 (never bypass — model reload → RAM-leak OOM, which crashed the Pod this session); lock a good base seed on a clean image first; test each LoRA in its native prompt format and check the creator's recommended base/precision (mismatch = flat). RELEASE TARGET ~25th June — ship the refined base (config now locked); add extend only if it lands clean in time.
